title: Block Structure: Lexical Scoping and Nesting
overview: The organization of code into nested blocks that define scope and lifetime. A foundational concept for structured programming.
historical_context: Introduced by ALGOL 58 and refined in ALGOL 60, block structure replaced the linear, flat structure of assembly and FORTRAN.
mental_model: Think of Russian nesting dolls. Each block creates a new environment that can access its own variables and those of its parents.
key_innovations: Variable lifetimes tied to block exit.
tradeoffs: Can lead to complex lookup rules and "shadowing" of variables.
legacy: The standard for almost all modern imperative and functional languages.
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Trace the evolution of block structure from ALGOL to C.
Compare block structure with the earlier GOTO-based control flow.
